claims.claim: We evaluate persisted per-tensor 8-bit stochastic gradient descent with momentum (8-bit SGDM) as a memory-efficient alternative to fp32 SGDM for LoRA adapter fine-tuning.

evidence_refs.quote: # Multi-seed AdamW-inclusive GPT-2-small LoRA validation for persisted 8-bit SGDM momentum

"claim_scope": "In a 3-seed GPT-2-small LoRA fine-tuning harness over Alice in Wonderland, Penn Treebank, and Tiny Shakespeare, persisted per-tensor 8-bit SGDM momentum matched fp32 SGDM validation loss within 0.00231 loss on every seed, outperformed no-momentum SGD and the configured AdamW baseline, and used about 25% of fp32 SGDM momentum-state bytes.",

"claim_scope": "In a 3-seed GPT-2-small LoRA fine-tuning harness over Alice in Wonderland, Penn Treebank, and Tiny Shakespeare, persisted per-tensor 8-bit SGDM momentum matched fp32 SGDM validation loss within 0.00231 loss on every seed, outperformed no-momentum SGD and the configured AdamW baseline, and used about 25% of fp32 SGDM momentum-state bytes.",

claims.claim: Optimizer-state bytes were 442,560 for 8-bit SGDM versus 1,769,472 for fp32 SGDM, a 4× reduction.
